entry.title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Illuminating Media

media:group.media:title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Illuminating Media
media:group.media:description:
Join us with Saelan Twerdy as we unbox Illuminating Media: Transmitting the Renaissance in England, 1400–1550.
Bridging medieval studies, media studies, design history, and communications, Illuminating Media highlights the surprising endurance of these visual strategies in contemporary culture, from the design of passports to the layout of currency. Written in crisp, engaging prose, the book offers a new way of seeing both medieval art and modern design, making a powerful case for the value of interdisciplinary scholarship.
This book is now available in print and open access at press.library.concordia.ca.

entry.title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Literary Life After Death

media:group.media:title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Literary Life After Death
media:group.media:description:
Join Ryan Van Huijstee for the unboxing of Literary Life After Death: Commemorations of Writers in English-Speaking Canada by Carole Gerson.
Edited by Gabriela Aceves Sepúlveda, Analys Álvarez Hernádez, and Zaira Zarza.
Commemorations of deceased writers can often take on a life of their own—from the stately and serious to the kitschy and commercial. In Literary Life After Death, Carole Gerson categorizes and analyzes the ways in which writers have been commemorated in Canada to show how tributes have changed over time and in response to evolving national and social concerns.
This book is now available in print and open access at press.library.concordia.ca.

entry.title: Accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés avec Nathalie Auger et Caroline Dault

media:group.media:title: Accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés avec Nathalie Auger et Caroline Dault
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour le deuxième événement de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
Nathalie Auger, U. de Montpellier Paul-Valéry, et Caroline Dault, U. De Moncton, se rassemble pour l’enregistrement en direct de l’ épisode du balado “Plurilinguisme en éducation”, accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés.
PLURI-ED-J-2 mets de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.

entry.title: Explore the Leonard & Bina Ellen Art Gallery Vault! #montreal #art #curator #canadianart #quebec

media:group.media:title: Explore the Leonard & Bina Ellen Art Gallery Vault! #montreal #art #curator #canadianart #quebec
media:group.media:description:
Julia Eilers Smith, Max Stern Curator of Research and Collection, and Nicole Burisch, Director, give us a closer look inside the vault of the Leonard & Bina Ellen Art Gallery!
From the recent episode in our Closer Look series, where 4th Space explores the spaces and projects across Concordia that you might not otherwise get a chance to see!
In case you missed it, you can explore the lab by clicking the link above 🔗

entry.title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation - Présentation avec Catherine Maynard

media:group.media:title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation - Présentation avec Catherine Maynard
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour la troisième présentation de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
• Diffuser des textes identitaires plurilingues dans le cadre d'une bibliothèque vivante: l'expérience de personnes immigrantes en processus d'alphabétisation.
PLURI-ED-J-2 mets de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.

entry.title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation 2026 - Allocution de bienvenue, présentations et balado

media:group.media:title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation 2026 - Allocution de bienvenue, présentations et balado
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour les premiers événements de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
00:13 Intro Espace 4 01:38 Intro Diane Querrien 05:38 Implantation d'une approche interlinguistique : mesures et apprentissages par Claude Quevillon Lacasse et Susan Ballinger 36:20 Accueillir les élèves nouvellement arrivés : enregistrement en direct par Nathalie Auger et Caroline Dault 1:27:37 Unlocking Plurilingual and Pluricultural Competence through the Action-Oriented Approach by Angelica Galante
PLURI-ED-J-2 mets de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.

entry.title: Defining Sustainability | Part of Serious Game Arcade: Sustainable Games

media:group.media:title: Defining Sustainability | Part of Serious Game Arcade: Sustainable Games
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Katie Roger, join us for the third panel of this year's Serious Game Arcade, which theme is Games and Sustainability, asking how games can seriously help to build sustainable and regenerative futures.
Speakers:
• Michael Iantorno • Rainforest Scully-Baker • Hussam Al Maleh
Serious games offer a chance to educate, express, and intervene, where play becomes a tool for comprehension and exploration.
Organized by Scott DeJong, Scott Preston, Kara Stone, and Mitchell Beer.

entry.title: Experimenting with Sustainability Through Play | Part of Serious Game Arcade: Sustainable Games

media:group.media:title: Experimenting with Sustainability Through Play | Part of Serious Game Arcade: Sustainable Games
media:group.media:description:
Join us for the second panel of this year's Serious Game Arcade, which theme is Games and Sustainability, asking how games can seriously help to build sustainable and regenerative futures.
Speakers:
• Bart Simon • Julius Lindsay • Milton Lim, • Gabrielle Trepanier Jobin
Serious games offer a chance to educate, express, and intervene, where play becomes a tool for comprehension and exploration.
Organized by Scott DeJong, Scott Preston, Kara Stone, and Mitchell Beer.

entry.title: Playing with Climate Education | Part of Serious Game Arcade: Sustainable Games

media:group.media:title: Playing with Climate Education | Part of Serious Game Arcade: Sustainable Games
media:group.media:description:
Join for the first panel of this year's Serious Game Arcade, which theme is Games and Sustainability, asking how games can seriously help to build sustainable and regenerative futures.
Speakers:
• Bart Simon • Julius Lindsay • Milton Lim • Gabrielle Trepanier Jobin
Serious games offer a chance to educate, express, and intervene, where play becomes a tool for comprehension and exploration.
Organized by Scott DeJong, Scott Preston, Kara Stone, and Mitchell Beer.

entry.title: The Growing Place of AI in Our World: Environmental, Ethical and Social Considerations

media:group.media:title: The Growing Place of AI in Our World: Environmental, Ethical and Social Considerations
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Damon Matthews, join us for a panel discussion about the environmental, ethical and societal implications of AI and its growing influence in our society.
Presenters:
• Genevieve Patterson • François Burra • Vijay Kolinjivadi • Tanja Tajmel
Part of LEADS Wraps Up!
LEADS is Leadership in Environmental and Digital innovation for Sustainability (LEADS) is an NSERC-CREATE graduate program on the theme of science leadership for global sustainability in the digital age.

media:group.media:title: Empowered Aging - Project Showcase
media:group.media:description:
Come see the projects that were carried out in the past two years through the Grace Dart Knowledge Mobilization Fund for Empowered Aging! 🎨🪴🩰
About the Award:
The Grace Dart Knowledge Mobilization Award matches students with community organizations and nonprofits working primarily with older adults. It is an opportunity to co-develop community-engaged activities with older adults and to acquire experience in the community sector
Empowered aging considers growing old as an active and evolving process wherein diverse individuals have opportunities to access resources and supports that will enable ongoing realization of personal potentials as needs and situations change over time.
A knowledge mobilization award is not a research award: it is an award to help make your research relevant to potential audiences. The goal of this award is to develop effective ways, using methods developed through your research, to engage older adults in topics that are important to them and to bring research knowledge and expertise to other sectors of society.
Organized by Eric Craven, engAGE: Centre for Research on Aging

entry.title: Empowered Aging - The Grace Dart Foundation Knowledge Mobilization Fund Showcase

media:group.media:title: Empowered Aging - The Grace Dart Foundation Knowledge Mobilization Fund Showcase
media:group.media:description:
Join us for a panel discussion to discover all the different projects from the different collaborations with students community groups and older adults through the 2024-2026 Grace Dart Foundation Knowledge Mobilization Fund for Empowered Aging. Learn more about game designs, digital maps, dance classes for fall prevention, VR worldmaking and more! 🎤
About the Award:
The Grace Dart Knowledge Mobilization Award matches students with community organizations and nonprofits working primarily with older adults. It is an opportunity to co-develop community-engaged activities with older adults and to acquire experience in the community sector
Empowered aging considers growing old as an active and evolving process wherein diverse individuals have opportunities to access resources and supports that will enable ongoing realization of personal potentials as needs and situations change over time.
A knowledge mobilization award is not a research award: it is an award to help make your research relevant to potential audiences. The goal of this award is to develop effective ways, using methods developed through your research, to engage older adults in topics that are important to them and to bring research knowledge and expertise to other sectors of society.
Organized by Eric Craven, engAGE: Centre for Research on Aging

entry.title: New Books in Canadian Literature | A Collective Book Launch

media:group.media:title: New Books in Canadian Literature | A Collective Book Launch
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Ryan Van Huijstee, join us as Concordia University Press and the Association of Canadian College and University Teachers in English celebrate recent works in the field of Canadian literature published by Canadian university presses. Authors in-person and online talked about their work, how they have engaged with Canadian literary history, and new directions emerging in literary studies. 📚
Speakers:
• Myra Bloom Evasive Manoeuvres: Canadian Women's Confessional Writing Myra Bloom is associate professor of English at Glendon College, York University.
• Lindsay Diehl Reading with My Grandmother: Chinese Canadian Literature, History, and Family Lindsay Diehl is an assistant professor of English at the University of Manitoba.
• Sandra Djwa Ground to Stand On: A Canadian Literary Life Sandra Djwa is professor emerita of English at Simon Fraser University.
• Carole Gerson Literary Life after Death: Commemorations of Writers in English-Speaking Canada Carole Gerson is professor emerita of English at Simon Fraser University.
• Jody Mason Books for Development: Canada in the Late Twentieth-Century World Jody Mason is professor of English at Carleton University.
• Kim Trainor Blue Thinks Itself Within Me: Lyric Poetry, Ecology, and Lichenous Form Kim Trainor has won the Gustafson Prize, The Malahat Review’s Long Poem Prize, and The Antigonish Review’s Great Blue Heron Poetry Prize. She lives in Vancouver.
• Ryan Van Huijstee (Moderator) Director of the Concordia University Press
With thanks to the support of staff from McGill-Queen’s University Press, Wilfrid Laurier University Press, and University of Regina Press.

entry.title: Shelter in Text: Essays on Dwelling and Refuge

media:group.media:title: Shelter in Text: Essays on Dwelling and Refuge
media:group.media:description:
Join us to celebrate the launch of Shelter in Text: Essays on Dwelling and Refuge (University of Alberta Press), edited by Myra Bloom and Kasia Van Schaik. The event will include presentations by contributors from Shelter in Text, followed by a Q and A.
Echoing the pandemic-era phrase “shelter in place,” and extending beyond it, Shelter in Text: Essays on Dwelling and Refuge examines how writing can create, illuminate, and complicate ideas about dwelling, belonging, or finding safe harbour. Through an engaging blend of academic essays and creative nonfiction, contributors interrogate the connections between the concepts of shelter and text, centering questions of care, disability, and housing inequality.
Speakers:
• Myra Bloom • Kasia Van Schaik • Jessi MacEachern • Kayla Penteliuk • Holly Vestad
Organized by Kasia Van Schaik and Myra Bloom.

entry.title: A Closer Look: The Bergdahl Lab | The Cardiovascular and Performance Physiology Lab

media:group.media:title: A Closer Look: The Bergdahl Lab | The Cardiovascular and Performance Physiology Lab
media:group.media:description:
Join us for the next instalment of tour Closer Look series - exploring the many labs and spaces across Concordia University, giving you a closer look at what happens here!
This session takes us to the HKAP Training Center with researchers working with the Bergdahl Cardiovascular and Performance Physiology Lab at Loyola to see inside the current work happening!
The Bergdahl laboratory research focuses on the integrative regulation of blood flow, mitochondrial biology, and cardiovascular performance, with particular emphasis on how nutrition, exercise, and metabolic stress drive functional adaptation. In addition to its work on occlusion-based training, a central objective of the program is cranberry polyphenol supplementation as a nutritional strategy to enhance performance and recovery. Their studies have shown that daily cranberry supplementation can improve muscle oxygenation, vascular function, and exercise tolerance, while attenuating exercise-induced lactate accumulation. Building on these findings, the Bergdahl lab has applied nutritional and blood flow restriction (BFR) interventions in recreational and elite athletes, older adults, frail cancer patients, individuals with long COVID, and people with Parkinson’s disease, integrating physiological testing, imaging, and biomarker analyses to investigate mechanisms underlying functional improvement.
Many Thanks to:
Andreas Bergdahl Antoine Denis Natasha Fedorsen Radu Urian
